category_title: Students who attempt to engage in peer-to-peer learning despite being positioned as work-avoidant
student_description: Mark is an active learner who is often mischaracterized as a behavior problem due to his out-of-turn talking, which he actually uses to help peers learn. He completely disengages and becomes socially distant when teachers do the work for him or police his behavior over providing meaningful instruction. He uniquely needs accessible texts and the autonomy to collaborate and take charge of his own learning.

criteria.rationale: Mark needs texts and tasks kept in his 'mid-range' of reading to maintain engagement and prevent off-task behavior.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan includes consistently selecting or adapting texts and materials to match Mark's accessible reading level.

criteria.rationale: Mark thrives and views himself as an agentic learner when given regular opportunities to work with and help his peers.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term strategy incorporates structured, ongoing opportunities for peer collaboration and group work.
